# Liu Yuchen (Lyrica)

> Independent researcher in mechanistic interpretability and methodology for vision-language-action (VLA) models. Founder of Φ(fight) Research. HKUST EE+AI sophomore. Four papers under double-blind review at NeurIPS 2026.

## Identity

- **Full name:** Liu Yuchen
- **Alternate names:** Lyrica, kaminoguo (GitHub handle)
- **Pronouns:** he/him
- **Affiliation:** Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST), EE + AI dual major, sophomore
- **Role:** Founder, Φ(fight) Research (May 2026 – present)
- **Email:** yuchen@φ.monster (technical: yuchen@xn--7xa.monster)
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/kaminoguo
- **Personal site:** https://lyrica.φ.monster (technical: https://lyrica.xn--7xa.monster)
- **Φ org:** https://φ.monster

## Research

### Current focus

- Mechanistic interpretability for vision-language-action models
- Adversarial multi-agent embodied AI (VLA-vs-VLA)
- Self-play policies and circuit-level analysis of exploit-prone subnetworks
- World models under adversarial dynamics

### Four papers under double-blind review at NeurIPS 2026

Three sole-author, one co-first. Titles and anonymous repository identifiers are withheld until NeurIPS 2026 decisions land in September 2026 to preserve double-blind review integrity. Full information available on request after the review period.

Topic descriptions (review-safe):

1. **Sole author.** Mechanistic interpretability of transformer language models — shows that a class of widely-used spectral diagnostics on hidden states is systematically biased by an input-invariant high-variance direction, and proposes a one-line correction.
2. **Sole author.** Activation-ablation methodology for Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models — demonstrates that the standard ablation primitive overstates per-layer importance, introduces a corrected protocol implemented as forward-hook context managers, with closed-loop validation in a standard robotic simulator.
3. **Sole author.** Cross-view attention analysis in VLAs — quantitative framework relating attention partition to behavioral multi-view fusion.
4. **Co-first author.** Deployment-failure audit suite for VLAs — mutually-exclusive failure-mode taxonomy with calibrated confidence intervals.

### Three papers in progress for ICLR 2027

With Han Muchen, under Φ(fight) Research:
1. Φ-Arena (co-led) — first open benchmark for VLA-vs-VLA evaluation under contact-rich physics
2. Mechanistic interpretability of self-play policies (lead)
3. Energy-bounded adversarial games (collaboration with Han Muchen)

## Pre-research projects

- **Squirrel** (Dec 2025 – Feb 2026): AI memory layer for coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex). Rust daemon + MCP server + Python service for LLM-based memory extraction. Archived at ~1,000+ GitHub stars when pivoted to research.
- **OfferI** (Feb 2025 – Dec 2025): AI study-abroad school selection agent for international students. Closed.
- **Xiaoniao** (Aug – Sep 2025): Cross-platform translation tool, Go (Chrome extension + Windows app, three AI provider integrations).

## Technical stack

- **Languages:** Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go (proficient)
- **Research tools:** PyTorch + HuggingFace Transformers / datasets / accelerate
- **Hands-on VLA models:** OpenVLA family, π₀, X-VLA, SmolVLA
- **Simulators:** MuJoCo, Isaac Sim, Genesis, LIBERO
- **Statistical methods:** Wilson confidence intervals, cluster bootstrap, activation interventions, forward-hook analysis
- **Hardware:** LeKiwi mobile manipulator, dual-arm bimanual setups, embedded systems

## Education

Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, Sep 2024 – 2029 (expected). Electronic Engineering + Artificial Intelligence dual major. Top 3 in COMP6211 (PhD-level AI Startup Course), Summer 2025.

## What I'm not

- Not affiliated with any big AI lab (Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepMind, etc.)
- Not raising VC, not building a startup
- Not pursuing PhD (yet — open question for Year 2-3)
